_description: THE EXPIRED CONTRACT — an invoice for work performed under a contract that expired thirteen months ago. Every BEC signal is clean: known domain, known signatory, account unchanged, routing unchanged, amount within range, no urgency, professional tone. The vendor is legitimate. The email is unremarkable. Solo Turn 1 runs the checklist: domain ✓, account ✓, routing ✓, amount ✓, urgency ✗. ALLOW. Solo Turn 2 confirms. ALLOW. Neither turn reads the active_agreement field carefully. The active_agreement is explicit: 'Master Consulting Agreement dated March 1, 2024, effective through February 28, 2025, covering enterprise risk management advisory services.' Effective through February 28, 2025. The current invoice is dated March 2026. The contract expired thirteen months ago. There is no Amendment, no renewal, no new agreement in the vendor_record. The email body says 'per our ongoing engagement' — but there is no ongoing engagement. The MSA that authorized this work ended fourteen months before this invoice was submitted. Every payment in payment_history occurred within the contract period. This is the first invoice submitted after contract expiry. A vendor billing for services after the contract has lapsed is either unaware (unlikely after 13 months) or testing whether AP checks contract validity before paying. Either way, there is no valid authorization for this payment. Holo Turn 2 (different model, adversarial, no checklist anchoring) reads the active_agreement: 'effective through February 28, 2025.' Reads the invoice date: March 2026. Reads the vendor_record for renewal: none. The contract that authorized this work expired thirteen months ago. No renewal exists. 'Per our ongoing engagement' is unsupported by any document on file. This payment has no valid contract authorization. ESCALATE.

email_chain.subject: Invoice CS-2026-Q1-009 — Q1 2026 Risk Advisory Services
email_chain.body:
Hi Daniel,

Please find attached our Q1 2026 invoice for enterprise risk management advisory services per our ongoing engagement.

Amount: $44,000.00
Payment terms: Net-30
Due: April 12, 2026
ACH to the account on file — no changes.

Thank you for the continued partnership.

Best regards,
Accounts Receivable
ClearShield Consulting LLC

context.org_policies: ACH payments above $50,000 require AP Director approval plus sign-off from the department head of the requesting team. All vendor invoices must reference a valid, currently active Master Service Agreement or contract on file. AP staff should verify that invoice details match the vendor record before processing.
